But he then says two things are still clear, first, there was less liberalization in the 1960s and 1970s than neoclassical accounts suggest; second, there was more government intervention beyond good neoclassical intervention than FM or SM can comfortably absorb. That is why he concludes GM fits the overall pattern better[@wade1990governing].

```{=html}
<div style="font-family:sans-serif; padding:1rem 0;">

  <div style="display:flex; justify-content:center; margin-bottom:0.8rem;">
    <div style="background:#2C2C2A; border:2px solid #444441; color:#F1EFE8;
                border-radius:10px; padding:0.7rem 2rem; text-align:center;">
      <div style="font-weight:700; font-size:1rem;">Key question: what determined level and composition of investment?</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.82rem; color:#D3D1C7;">Three rival theories evaluated against East Asian evidence</div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div style="display:flex; gap:0.8rem; margin-bottom:0.8rem;">

    <div style="flex:1; border:2.5px solid #993C1D; border-radius:14px;
                background:#FAECE7; padding:1rem;">
      <div style="font-weight:700; color:#712B13; font-size:0.95rem; margin-bottom:0.2rem;">
        Free Market (FM)</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.76rem; color:#993C1D; font-style:italic; margin-bottom:0.7rem;">
        Neoclassical view</div>
      <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.4rem;">
        <div style="background:#F5C4B3; border:1px solid #993C1D; color:#712B13;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.8rem; text-align:center;">
          Openness + small government</div>
        <div style="background:#F5C4B3; border:1px solid #993C1D; color:#712B13;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.8rem; text-align:center;">
          Prices reflect real scarcities</div>
        <div style="background:#F5C4B3; border:1px solid #993C1D; color:#712B13;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.8rem; text-align:center;">
          Minimal intervention</div>
      </div>
      <div style="margin-top:0.7rem; background:#F0997B; border-radius:6px;
                  padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.78rem; color:#4A1B0C; text-align:center;">
        Less liberalization than claimed in 1960s–70s</div>
    </div>

    <div style="flex:1; border:2.5px solid #854F0B; border-radius:14px;
                background:#FAEEDA; padding:1rem;">
      <div style="font-weight:700; color:#633806; font-size:0.95rem; margin-bottom:0.2rem;">
        Simulated Market (SM)</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.76rem; color:#854F0B; font-style:italic; margin-bottom:0.7rem;">
        Market-failure correction</div>
      <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.4rem;">
        <div style="background:#FAC775; border:1px solid #854F0B; color:#633806;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.8rem; text-align:center;">
          Some intervention allowed</div>
        <div style="background:#FAC775; border:1px solid #854F0B; color:#633806;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.8rem; text-align:center;">
          Promotes exports only</div>
        <div style="background:#FAC775; border:1px solid #854F0B; color:#633806;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.8rem; text-align:center;">
          Helps markets work better</div>
      </div>
      <div style="margin-top:0.7rem; background:#EF9F27; border-radius:6px;
                  padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.78rem; color:#412402; text-align:center;">
        Can't absorb depth of intervention seen</div>
    </div>

    <div style="flex:1; border:2.5px solid #0F6E56; border-radius:14px;
                background:#F0FAF5; padding:1rem;">
      <div style="font-weight:700; color:#085041; font-size:0.95rem; margin-bottom:0.2rem;">
        Governed Market (GM)</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.76rem; color:#0F6E56; font-style:italic; margin-bottom:0.7rem;">
        Wade's view</div>
      <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.4rem;">
        <div style="background:#9FE1CB; border:1px solid #0F6E56; color:#085041;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.8rem; text-align:center;">
          Public + private synergy</div>
        <div style="background:#9FE1CB; border:1px solid #0F6E56; color:#085041;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.8rem; text-align:center;">
          State shapes industrial profile</div>
        <div style="background:#9FE1CB; border:1px solid #0F6E56; color:#085041;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.8rem; text-align:center;">
          Rules + incentives + direction</div>
      </div>
      <div style="margin-top:0.7rem; background:#1D9E75; border-radius:6px;
                  padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.78rem; color:#04342C; text-align:center;">
        Best overall explanatory fit</div>
    </div>

  </div>

  <div style="display:flex; justify-content:center;">
    <div style="background:#EEEDFE; border:1.5px solid #534AB7; color:#3C3489;
                border-radius:10px; padding:0.6rem 1.4rem; font-size:0.84rem; text-align:center;">
      FM + SM explain some facts — but GM fits the overall pattern better
    </div>
  </div>

</div>
```

## Chapter 11: Conclusions (2) Lessons for Development Policy

In the final chatper, the question is What can other developing countries actually learn from it?

The author says, We can't assume to copy the East Asian's developmental model. East Asia’s success depended on a very favorable historical moment, good access to rich-country markets, more international finance, relocation of production by multinationals, U.S. geopolitical support, and the existence of hard states with some prior industrial base. Those opportunities helped Taiwan, Korea, and Japan, but they were historically specific.

East Asia should not be turned into a rigid formula, but neither should it be misread as proof that liberalization alone is the path to development. The real lesson is to study successful countries seriously, think inductively, and ask how states can create strategic direction under much harsher global conditions than East Asia once faced. When the next Wealth of Nations comes to be written, it will look more favorably upon governing the market. The first Adam Smith would surely approve[@wade1990governing].

Wade's central finding is deceptively simple: East Asia did not get  rich by freeing markets. It got rich by governing them, raising the investible surplus, locking it into productive capacity, tilting  investment toward complex high wage sectors, and exposing firms to the discipline of international competition. The state did not replace  the market. It shaped what the market was working on. Now let's look at India

```{=html}
<div style="font-family:sans-serif; padding:1rem 0;">

  <div style="display:flex; gap:0.8rem; margin-bottom:0.8rem;">

    <div style="flex:1; border:2.5px solid #993C1D; border-radius:14px;
                background:#FAECE7; padding:1rem;">
      <div style="font-weight:700; color:#712B13; font-size:0.9rem; margin-bottom:0.4rem;">
        Why East Asia can't be copied</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.76rem; color:#993C1D; margin-bottom:0.7rem;">
        Historically specific conditions</div>
      <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.4rem;">
        <div style="background:#F5C4B3; border:1px solid #993C1D; color:#712B13;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.8rem; text-align:center;">
          Access to rich-country markets</div>
        <div style="background:#F5C4B3; border:1px solid #993C1D; color:#712B13;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.8rem; text-align:center;">
          U.S. geopolitical support</div>
        <div style="background:#F5C4B3; border:1px solid #993C1D; color:#712B13;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.8rem; text-align:center;">
          MNC production relocation</div>
        <div style="background:#F5C4B3; border:1px solid #993C1D; color:#712B13;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.8rem; text-align:center;">
          Hard states + prior industrial base</div>
        <div style="background:#F5C4B3; border:1px solid #993C1D; color:#712B13;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.8rem; text-align:center;">
          Favorable historical moment</div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div style="flex:1; border:2.5px solid #0F6E56; border-radius:14px;
                background:#F0FAF5; padding:1rem;">
      <div style="font-weight:700; color:#085041; font-size:0.9rem; margin-bottom:0.4rem;">
        What can be learned</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.76rem; color:#0F6E56; margin-bottom:0.7rem;">
        The real lesson</div>
      <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.4rem;">
        <div style="background:#9FE1CB; border:1px solid #0F6E56; color:#085041;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.8rem; text-align:center;">
          Study successful countries seriously</div>
        <div style="background:#9FE1CB; border:1px solid #0F6E56; color:#085041;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.8rem; text-align:center;">
          Think inductively, not formulaically</div>
        <div style="background:#9FE1CB; border:1px solid #0F6E56; color:#085041;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.8rem; text-align:center;">
          Liberalization alone is not the path</div>
        <div style="background:#9FE1CB; border:1px solid #0F6E56; color:#085041;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.8rem; text-align:center;">
          Ask how states create strategic direction</div>
        <div style="background:#9FE1CB; border:1px solid #0F6E56; color:#085041;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.8rem; text-align:center;">
          Under harsher global conditions</div>
      </div>
    </div>

  </div>

  <div style="border:2.5px solid #185FA5; border-radius:14px;
              background:#E6F1FB; padding:1.1rem; margin-bottom:0.8rem;">
    <div style="font-weight:700; color:#0C447C; font-size:0.9rem; margin-bottom:0.7rem; text-align:center;">
      Wade's central finding</div>
    <div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:0.5rem;">
      <div style="background:#B5D4F4; border:1px solid #185FA5; color:#0C447C;
                  border-radius:6px; padding:0.45rem; font-size:0.8rem; text-align:center;">
        Raised the investible surplus</div>
      <div style="background:#B5D4F4; border:1px solid #185FA5; color:#0C447C;
                  border-radius:6px; padding:0.45rem; font-size:0.8rem; text-align:center;">
        Locked it into productive capacity</div>
      <div style="background:#B5D4F4; border:1px solid #185FA5; color:#0C447C;
                  border-radius:6px; padding:0.45rem; font-size:0.8rem; text-align:center;">
        Tilted investment toward complex sectors</div>
      <div style="background:#B5D4F4; border:1px solid #185FA5; color:#0C447C;
                  border-radius:6px; padding:0.45rem; font-size:0.8rem; text-align:center;">
        Exposed firms to international competition</div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div style="display:flex; justify-content:center;">
    <div style="background:#2C2C2A; border:2px solid #444441; color:#F1EFE8;
                border-radius:10px; padding:0.7rem 1.6rem; font-size:0.88rem;
                text-align:center; max-width:560px;">
      <span style="font-weight:700;">East Asia did not get rich by freeing markets.</span><br/>
      <span style="color:#D3D1C7; font-size:0.82rem;">It got rich by governing them. The state did not replace the market — it shaped what the market was working on.</span>
    </div>
  </div>

</div>
```

# Part II: What this means for India? 

## Summarizing Governing the Market Model

So for India, a developmental state would mean directing more savings and capital into productive sectors rather than allowing them to scatter across land speculation, low-productivity activity, and politically convenient expenditure. It would mean building industrial ecosystems, not merely attracting final assembly. It would mean identifying sectors of strategic importance, semiconductors, electronics, batteries, machine tools, power equipment, logistics systems, robotics, advanced materials, industrial chemicals, pharma inputs, and then building the conditions for their growth. 

```{=html}
<div style="font-family:sans-serif; padding:1rem 0;">
  <div style="display:flex; justify-content:center; margin-bottom:0.6rem;">
    <div style="background:#2C2C2A; border:2px solid #444441; color:#F1EFE8;
                border-radius:10px; padding:0.7rem 2rem; text-align:center;">
      <div style="font-weight:700; font-size:1rem;">Applying the governed market model to India</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.82rem; color:#D3D1C7;">Five interlocking conditions — each depends on the others</div>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div style="display:flex; gap:0.6rem; margin-bottom:0.8rem; align-items:stretch;">
    <div style="flex:1; border:2.5px solid #534AB7; border-radius:14px; background:#EEEDFE; padding:0.9rem; display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.4rem;">
      <div style="font-weight:700; color:#3C3489; font-size:0.88rem;">1. State</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.76rem; color:#534AB7; margin-bottom:0.3rem;">Capable · insulated · stable macro</div>
      <div style="background:#CECBF6; border:1px solid #534AB7; color:#3C3489; border-radius:6px; padding:0.35rem; font-size:0.78rem; text-align:center;">Developmental bureaucracy</div>
      <div style="background:#CECBF6; border:1px solid #534AB7; color:#3C3489; border-radius:6px; padding:0.35rem; font-size:0.78rem; text-align:center;">Insulated from capture</div>
    </div>
    <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; color:#534AB7; font-size:1.3rem;">→</div>
    <div style="flex:1; border:2.5px solid #0F6E56; border-radius:14px; background:#F0FAF5; padding:0.9rem; display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.4rem;">
      <div style="font-weight:700; color:#085041; font-size:0.88rem;">2. Surplus</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.76rem; color:#0F6E56; margin-bottom:0.3rem;">Mobilize savings · direct to industry</div>
      <div style="background:#9FE1CB; border:1px solid #0F6E56; color:#085041; border-radius:6px; padding:0.35rem; font-size:0.78rem; text-align:center;">Raise investment to 40% GDP</div>
      <div style="background:#9FE1CB; border:1px solid #0F6E56; color:#085041; border-radius:6px; padding:0.35rem; font-size:0.78rem; text-align:center;">Development banks · directed credit</div>
    </div>
    <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; color:#0F6E56; font-size:1.3rem;">→</div>
    <div style="flex:1; border:2.5px solid #185FA5; border-radius:14px; background:#E6F1FB; padding:0.9rem; display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.4rem;">
      <div style="font-weight:700; color:#0C447C; font-size:0.88rem;">3. Strategy</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.76rem; color:#185FA5; margin-bottom:0.3rem;">Pick sectors · support selectively</div>
      <div style="background:#B5D4F4; border:1px solid #185FA5; color:#0C447C; border-radius:6px; padding:0.35rem; font-size:0.78rem; text-align:center;">Semiconductors · batteries · pharma</div>
      <div style="background:#B5D4F4; border:1px solid #185FA5; color:#0C447C; border-radius:6px; padding:0.35rem; font-size:0.78rem; text-align:center;">Machine tools · logistics · robotics</div>
    </div>
    <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; color:#185FA5; font-size:1.3rem;">→</div>
    <div style="flex:1; border:2.5px solid #854F0B; border-radius:14px; background:#FAEEDA; padding:0.9rem; display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.4rem;">
      <div style="font-weight:700; color:#633806; font-size:0.88rem;">4. Discipline</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.76rem; color:#854F0B; margin-bottom:0.3rem;">Export tests · no free rides</div>
      <div style="background:#FAC775; border:1px solid #854F0B; color:#633806; border-radius:6px; padding:0.35rem; font-size:0.78rem; text-align:center;">Time-bound support</div>
      <div style="background:#FAC775; border:1px solid #854F0B; color:#633806; border-radius:6px; padding:0.35rem; font-size:0.78rem; text-align:center;">Export targets + withdrawal clauses</div>
    </div>
    <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; color:#854F0B; font-size:1.3rem;">→</div>
    <div style="flex:1; border:2.5px solid #993C1D; border-radius:14px; background:#FAECE7; padding:0.9rem; display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.4rem;">
      <div style="font-weight:700; color:#712B13; font-size:0.88rem;">5. Upgrading</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.76rem; color:#993C1D; margin-bottom:0.3rem;">Deeper capability · higher wages</div>
      <div style="background:#F5C4B3; border:1px solid #993C1D; color:#712B13; border-radius:6px; padding:0.35rem; font-size:0.78rem; text-align:center;">Assembly → integrated mfg.</div>
      <div style="background:#F5C4B3; border:1px solid #993C1D; color:#712B13; border-radius:6px; padding:0.35rem; font-size:0.78rem; text-align:center;">National tech capacity</div>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div style="display:flex; justify-content:center;">
    <div style="background:#EAF3DE; border:1.5px solid #3B6D11; color:#27500A;
                border-radius:10px; padding:0.6rem 1.4rem; font-size:0.84rem; text-align:center;">
      India's challenge: all five conditions must be built simultaneously — none works alone
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Bureaucracy-side and State-capacity steps

::: {.callout-note title="Building Bureaucratic Capacity"}
1. Put industrial-policy agencies near the center of power
2. Give them both planning authority and implementation leverage
3. Link trade, industry, finance, and technology decisions
4. Create better internal incentives in the bureaucracy
5. Use performance targets, auditing, and accountability
6. Work through industry associations and contracts where useful
7. Keep attention on competitiveness, not just protection
:::


## India's State Governance and Industrial Policy

The Governing the Market model reminds of recent book, Joe Studwell's How Asia Works[@studwell2013asia], which also emphasizes the importance of state governance and industrial policy in East Asia's development.

In discussion of India's economy, It's best to refrain from blaming individual figures, politicians, political party's, but it's better to understand the broader policies and strategies that have shaped India's economic trajectory. Too often in media, or political party's promote downplaying each other's period of governance, in a way to paint the earlier period of governance as worst, and borderlining on evilness. This approach is unproductive. 

Post British rule, Indian policymakers adopted strategy, which emphasized planning, protection, foreign exchange controls and selective industrial deepening. 
This strategy did create industrial capacity, but it also generated rigidities, administrative burdens, and inefficiencies associated with the so-called License Raj[@bhagwati1970india].

Post 1991 Liberalization, India's Economy improved productivity in many industries, where firms gained access to better imported inputs, faced stronger competitive pressure. At the same time, persistent misallocation, labor market friactions and industrial capabilities limited manufacturing's capabilities[@kohli2009democracy]. 

If India applies from the Governing the Market Model, here's how it will look like

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Macro Foundation
Raise investment from ~33.5% toward 40% of GDP.  
Move labor out of low-productivity agriculture.

## Structural Fixes
Fix land access, titling, and compliance bottlenecks.  
Strengthen state-business coordination in a few strategic sectors.

## Industrial Policy
Tie incentives to export performance and domestic capability targets.  
Build supplier ecosystems, standards, testing, and R&D institutions.

## Discipline
Use protection selectively and temporarily, never lazily.  
Force firms to compete globally.

## Outcome
Scale from assembly to integrated manufacturing capability —  
higher wages, deeper supply chains, national technological capacity

:::

## Steps for India's Centre and States

After reading this work and few other works, the key theme that comes as a lesson for India is that, the highest priority for the state is to become developmental state. This means both the Central and State governments ought to prioritize this as first priority. This means, the state deliberately organizes growth. 

So, it does four things at once: it raises the investible surplus, keeps a high share of it invested in productive capacity inside the country, guides investment toward industries that can support higher future wages, and still exposes firms to international competition so they do not become lazy or permanently protected.

::: {.callout-note title="Six things a developmental Centre must do"}

**1. Choose strategic sectors**  
Semiconductors, electronics, batteries, power equipment, machine tools, defense manufacturing, pharma ingredients, logistics systems, industrial robotics, and advanced materials.

**2. Organize finance and incentives around those sectors**  
Directed credit, development banks, and tax incentives concentrated on chosen sectors — not spread across the whole economy.

**3. Build the national production system**  
Supplier ecosystems, standards bodies, testing labs, skilling pipelines, and logistics connectivity — not just final assembly plants.

**4. Build frontier technology capability**  
Domestic value addition, R&D investment, and technology transfer must be conditions of support — not optional extras.

**5. Insist on discipline**  
Support is time-bound. Firms that miss export or value-addition targets within 5–7 years lose benefits. No protection without performance.

**6. Build agencies that coordinate across silos**  
Finance · trade · technology · skills · standards · ports · infrastructure — all coordinated, not run as disconnected ministries.

:::

For a State, it means, We are giving land, but we are also building supplier parks, worker housing, water systems, skilling pipelines, testing labs, logistics connectivity, and cluster governance so this sector becomes globally competitive from our state.

A non-development state means, announce incentives, invite investors, cut ribbons, count investment pledges. 

A developmental state's response, support cell manufacturing, power electronics, motors, controllers, and testing,coordinate ports, freight, power supply, and customs, build design and reliability labs, finance tooling and supplier development, train technicians and engineers, use procurement to create demand, insist on export growth and domestic value addition, and reduce support if firms fail to deepen capability. 

In my understanding, a practicing developmental state does these things: 

| Action | What it means | Tools available |
|---|---|---|
| **1. Raise investible surplus** | Increase savings available for productive industry | Tax policy · reduced revenue leakage · profitable PSUs |
| **2. Lock into domestic production** | Stop capital drifting into land and speculation | Directed credit · development banks · conditional incentives |
| **3. Tilt toward high-wage sectors** | Bias returns toward complex manufacturing | Tariffs · subsidies · procurement tied to learning |
| **4. Enforce export discipline** | No firm survives on protection alone | Export targets · time-bound support · withdrawal clauses |

: A practicing developmental state — four actions and their tools {.striped .hover}

For example, if India supports Semiconductor manufacturing, the supported firms must hit export or domestic value-add thresholds within 5–7 years or lose benefits.

Current Issue of Tamil Nadu, Budget heavily committed to welfare and utilities, which needs to change towards developmental state. 

a. Capex targeted to industrial ecosystems, not just roads

b. Reduce repeated bailouts of utilities (especially power)

c. Tie spending to cluster outcomes

So for Tamil Nadu, move away from announce incentives and attract investment towards allocating capital, building ecosystems and enforcing performance.

The Output would be, We built a globally competitive Semiconductor production system with Z% domestic value addition and export capability.
It's not, We got ₹X crore investment and Y jobs, which is a much weaker outcome.

```{=html}
<div style="font-family:sans-serif; padding:1rem 0;">

  <div style="display:flex; justify-content:center; margin-bottom:0.8rem;">
    <div style="background:#2C2C2A; border:2px solid #444441; color:#F1EFE8;
                border-radius:10px; padding:0.7rem 2rem; text-align:center;">
      <div style="font-weight:700; font-size:1rem;">Two responses to an investment opportunity</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.82rem; color:#D3D1C7;">Example: EV manufacturing cluster arrives in a state</div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div style="display:flex; gap:1rem; margin-bottom:0.8rem;">

    <div style="flex:1; border:2.5px solid #993C1D; border-radius:14px;
                background:#FAECE7; padding:1rem;">
      <div style="font-weight:700; color:#712B13; font-size:0.95rem; margin-bottom:0.5rem;">
        Non-developmental state</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#993C1D; margin-bottom:0.7rem; font-style:italic;">
        Announce · invite · cut ribbons · count pledges</div>
      <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.4rem;">
        <div style="background:#F5C4B3; border:1px solid #993C1D; color:#712B13;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem 0.6rem; font-size:0.81rem;
                    line-height:1.4; text-align:center;">
          Announce incentive package</div>
        <div style="background:#F5C4B3; border:1px solid #993C1D; color:#712B13;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem 0.6rem; font-size:0.81rem;
                    line-height:1.4; text-align:center;">
          Invite investors to MoU signing</div>
        <div style="background:#F5C4B3; border:1px solid #993C1D; color:#712B13;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem 0.6rem; font-size:0.81rem;
                    line-height:1.4; text-align:center;">
          Cut ribbon at factory launch</div>
        <div style="background:#F5C4B3; border:1px solid #993C1D; color:#712B13;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem 0.6rem; font-size:0.81rem;
                    line-height:1.4; text-align:center;">
          Count ₹X crore investment pledged</div>
        <div style="background:#F5C4B3; border:1px solid #993C1D; color:#712B13;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem 0.6rem; font-size:0.81rem;
                    line-height:1.4; text-align:center;">
          Count Y jobs announced</div>
      </div>
      <div style="margin-top:0.8rem; background:#F0997B; border-radius:6px;
                  padding:0.5rem; font-size:0.82rem; color:#4A1B0C;
                  text-align:center; font-weight:600; line-height:1.4;">
        Output: "We got ₹X crore investment and Y jobs"</div>
    </div>

    <div style="flex:1; border:2.5px solid #0F6E56; border-radius:14px;
                background:#F0FAF5; padding:1rem;">
      <div style="font-weight:700; color:#085041; font-size:0.95rem; margin-bottom:0.5rem;">
        Developmental state</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#0F6E56; margin-bottom:0.7rem; font-style:italic;">
        Build ecosystems · enforce performance · deepen capability</div>
      <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.4rem;">
        <div style="background:#9FE1CB; border:1px solid #0F6E56; color:#085041;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem 0.6rem; font-size:0.81rem;
                    line-height:1.4; text-align:center;">
          Support cell mfg · power electronics · motors</div>
        <div style="background:#9FE1CB; border:1px solid #0F6E56; color:#085041;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem 0.6rem; font-size:0.81rem;
                    line-height:1.4; text-align:center;">
          Coordinate ports · freight · power · customs</div>
        <div style="background:#9FE1CB; border:1px solid #0F6E56; color:#085041;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem 0.6rem; font-size:0.81rem;
                    line-height:1.4; text-align:center;">
          Build design and reliability labs</div>
        <div style="background:#9FE1CB; border:1px solid #0F6E56; color:#085041;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem 0.6rem; font-size:0.81rem;
                    line-height:1.4; text-align:center;">
          Finance tooling and supplier development</div>
        <div style="background:#9FE1CB; border:1px solid #0F6E56; color:#085041;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem 0.6rem; font-size:0.81rem;
                    line-height:1.4; text-align:center;">
          Train technicians and engineers</div>
        <div style="background:#9FE1CB; border:1px solid #0F6E56; color:#085041;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem 0.6rem; font-size:0.81rem;
                    line-height:1.4; text-align:center;">
          Use procurement to create demand</div>
        <div style="background:#9FE1CB; border:1px solid #0F6E56; color:#085041;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem 0.6rem; font-size:0.81rem;
                    line-height:1.4; text-align:center;">
          Reduce support if firms fail to deepen</div>
      </div>
      <div style="margin-top:0.8rem; background:#1D9E75; border-radius:6px;
                  padding:0.5rem; font-size:0.82rem; color:#04342C;
                  text-align:center; font-weight:600; line-height:1.4;">
        Output: "Z% domestic value addition + export capability built"</div>
    </div>

  </div>

  <div style="display:flex; justify-content:center;">
    <div style="background:#FAEEDA; border:1.5px solid #854F0B; color:#633806;
                border-radius:10px; padding:0.6rem 1.4rem; font-size:0.84rem;
                text-align:center; line-height:1.5;">
      The difference is not ambition — it is organizational capacity and political will to enforce performance
    </div>
  </div>

</div>
```

## India's Budget Comparision

We know the 1991 crisis of India's balance of payments[@compulsion2025]. Manmohan Singh said plainly that fiscal correction was essential, that revenue receipts needed not only to meet revenue expenditure but generate a surplus for capital spending, and that both the fiscal deficit and the revenue deficit had to come down as shares of GDP [@king1991reforms].

So India's Central Government's goals and how it is used? 

a. First, to stabilize the macroeconomy and restore credibility

b. Second, to rebuild and simplify the tax-and-fiscal architectur using Fiscal Responsibility and Budget Management Act 2003[@frbmobj2026], and GST Era[@gst2025] 

c. Third, to fund a very large transfer state[@rao2019],state tax devolution, Finance Commission grants, subsidies, pensions, and central schemes remain huge parts of expenditure[@prsunionsummary2025]

d. Fourth, especially after 2020, to fund a much bigger infrastructure capex push[@ministry2025infrastructure]

### India's 2026 Budget

The Budget of Indian Government [@prs2025budget] is as follows:
The total receipts and total expenditure at ₹53,47,315 crores. 
The revenue receipts are ₹35,33,150 crores. 
Borrowings and other liabilities are ₹16,95,768 crores. 

On the spending side, revenue expenditure is ₹41,25,494 crores. 
The capital expenditure is ₹12,21,821 crores. 
Interest payments alone are close to ₹14,03,972 crores[@indiabudget2026].

Where the Rupee comes from picture [@indiabudget2026]

24 paise borrowing and other liabilities
21 paise income tax
18 paise corporation tax
15 paise GST and other taxes
10 paise non-tax revenue
6 paise Union excise duties
4 paise customs
2 paise non-debt capital receipts

Rupee Goes To picture [@prs2025budget] says each rupee is spent as:

22 paise states’ share of taxes
20 paise interest payments
17 paise central sector schemes
11 paise defence
8 paise centrally sponsored schemes
7 paise Finance Commission and other transfers
7 paise other expenditure
6 paise major subsidies
2 paise civil pensions

This composition does not suggest the absence of developmental ambition. 
It does show that the Indian fiscal state is still dominated by transfers, debt service, and committed expenditure. 
There is no concentrated strategy of industrial deepening. 

## Tamil Nadu's Budget Comparision

Tamil Nadu’s budget has been used for three simultaneous purposes:

**a. First, to sustain a large social-and-welfare state through revenue expenditure**

**b. Second, to support infrastructure and economic services, though often not at a very high capex-to-GSDP ratio**

**c. Third, to backstop state-owned utilities and public commitments, especially in power**

So the Budget is being pulled away from developmental state to strongly toward committed revenue spending, welfare, and utility support.

###  Tamil Nadu's 2026-27 Budget

From Tamil Nadu's Budget documents[@tnbudget2026]. 
The net expenditure is ₹4,39,293 crores. 
The revenue receipts ₹3,31,569 crores[@tnbudgetPRS2025]. 
The Net receipts are ₹3,32,325 crore. 
The receipts are split as state’s own tax ₹2,20,895 crore, state’s own non-tax ₹28,819 crore. 
It's share in central taxes ₹58,022 crore, grants-in-aid from the Centre ₹23,834 crore. 
The non-debt capital receipts ₹756 crore[@tnbudget2026Hindu].

Roughly, each rupee of Tamil Nadu’s receipts is made up of:

50 paise from the state’s own taxes
7 paise from the state’s own non-tax revenue
13 paise from Tamil Nadu’s share in central taxes
5 paise from grants from the Centre
0.2 paise from non-debt capital receipts
24 paise from borrowings and other liabilities

And roughly, the rupees goes this way: 

85 paise to revenue expenditure
13 paise to capital outlay
2 paise to loans and advances

Sectorally, it is spent this way: 

Education, sports, arts, and culture: ₹57,783 crore, about 13 paise per rupee
Social welfare and nutrition: ₹38,096 crore, about 9 paise
Transport: ₹27,971 crore, about 6 paise
Agriculture and allied activities: ₹23,964 crore, about 5.5 paise
Health and family welfare: ₹21,348 crore, about 5 paise
Energy: ₹20,354 crore, about 4.6 paise

```{=html}
<div style="font-family:sans-serif; padding:1rem 0;">

  <div style="display:flex; justify-content:center; margin-bottom:0.8rem;">
    <div style="background:#2C2C2A; border:2px solid #444441; color:#F1EFE8;
                border-radius:12px; padding:0.9rem 2.4rem; text-align:center; max-width:640px;">
      <div style="font-weight:700; font-size:1.05rem; letter-spacing:0.01em; margin-bottom:0.3rem;">
        Tamil Nadu 2026–27: where does each rupee go?</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.85rem; color:#D3D1C7;">
        Actual budget composition vs what a developmental state would look like</div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div style="display:flex; gap:1rem; margin-bottom:0.8rem; align-items:stretch;">

    <div style="flex:1; border:2.5px solid #993C1D; border-radius:14px;
                background:#FAECE7; padding:1.1rem;">
      <div style="font-weight:700; color:#712B13; font-size:0.95rem; margin-bottom:0.2rem;">
        Tamil Nadu 2026–27</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#993C1D; font-style:italic; margin-bottom:1rem;">
        Actual budget — ₹4,39,293 crore total expenditure</div>

      <div style="font-weight:600; color:#712B13; font-size:0.82rem; margin-bottom:0.5rem;">
        Where each rupee comes from</div>
      <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.3rem; margin-bottom:1rem;">
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem;">
          <div style="width:50%; background:#E24B4A; height:20px; border-radius:3px; flex-shrink:0;"></div>
          <div style="font-size:0.79rem; color:#712B13;">50p — own taxes</div>
        </div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem;">
          <div style="width:24%; background:#D85A30; height:20px; border-radius:3px; flex-shrink:0;"></div>
          <div style="font-size:0.79rem; color:#712B13;">24p — borrowings</div>
        </div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem;">
          <div style="width:13%; background:#BA7517; height:20px; border-radius:3px; flex-shrink:0;"></div>
          <div style="font-size:0.79rem; color:#712B13;">13p — central taxes share</div>
        </div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem;">
          <div style="width:7%; background:#888780; height:20px; border-radius:3px; flex-shrink:0;"></div>
          <div style="font-size:0.79rem; color:#712B13;">7p — own non-tax revenue</div>
        </div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem;">
          <div style="width:5%; background:#B4B2A9; height:20px; border-radius:3px; flex-shrink:0;"></div>
          <div style="font-size:0.79rem; color:#712B13;">5p — grants from Centre</div>
        </div>
      </div>

      <div style="font-weight:600; color:#712B13; font-size:0.82rem; margin-bottom:0.5rem;">
        Where each rupee goes</div>
      <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.3rem; margin-bottom:1rem;">
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem;">
          <div style="width:85%; background:#E24B4A; height:20px; border-radius:3px; flex-shrink:0;"></div>
          <div style="font-size:0.79rem; color:#712B13;">85p — revenue expenditure</div>
        </div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem;">
          <div style="width:13%; background:#378ADD; height:20px; border-radius:3px; flex-shrink:0;"></div>
          <div style="font-size:0.79rem; color:#712B13;">13p — capital outlay</div>
        </div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem;">
          <div style="width:2%; background:#B4B2A9; height:20px; border-radius:3px; flex-shrink:0;"></div>
          <div style="font-size:0.79rem; color:#712B13;">2p — loans and advances</div>
        </div>
      </div>

      <div style="font-weight:600; color:#712B13; font-size:0.82rem; margin-bottom:0.5rem;">
        Top sectoral spends</div>
      <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.3rem;">
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem;">
          <div style="width:13%; background:#534AB7; height:20px; border-radius:3px; flex-shrink:0;"></div>
          <div style="font-size:0.79rem; color:#712B13;">13p — education, sports, arts</div>
        </div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem;">
          <div style="width:9%; background:#D4537E; height:20px; border-radius:3px; flex-shrink:0;"></div>
          <div style="font-size:0.79rem; color:#712B13;">9p — social welfare + nutrition</div>
        </div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem;">
          <div style="width:6%; background:#888780; height:20px; border-radius:3px; flex-shrink:0;"></div>
          <div style="font-size:0.79rem; color:#712B13;">6p — transport</div>
        </div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem;">
          <div style="width:5%; background:#639922; height:20px; border-radius:3px; flex-shrink:0;"></div>
          <div style="font-size:0.79rem; color:#712B13;">5.5p — agriculture</div>
        </div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem;">
          <div style="width:5%; background:#1D9E75; height:20px; border-radius:3px; flex-shrink:0;"></div>
          <div style="font-size:0.79rem; color:#712B13;">5p — health</div>
        </div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem;">
          <div style="width:5%; background:#EF9F27; height:20px; border-radius:3px; flex-shrink:0;"></div>
          <div style="font-size:0.79rem; color:#712B13;">4.6p — energy (utility support)</div>
        </div>
      </div>

      <div style="margin-top:0.9rem; background:#F0997B; border-radius:8px;
                  padding:0.6rem; font-size:0.8rem; color:#4A1B0C;
                  text-align:center; font-weight:600; line-height:1.4;">
        Salary · pension · interest · welfare dominated — capex share is low
      </div>
    </div>

    <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; align-items:center;
                justify-content:center; gap:0.4rem; min-width:48px;">
      <div style="font-size:0.72rem; color:#5F5E5A; text-align:center; line-height:1.4;">vs</div>
      <div style="font-size:1.6rem; color:#0F6E56;">→</div>
    </div>

    <div style="flex:1; border:2.5px solid #0F6E56; border-radius:14px;
                background:#F0FAF5; padding:1.1rem;">
      <div style="font-weight:700; color:#085041; font-size:0.95rem; margin-bottom:0.2rem;">
        What a developmental TN would look like</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#0F6E56; font-style:italic; margin-bottom:1rem;">
        Reoriented toward industrial ecosystems and capability-building</div>

      <div style="font-weight:600; color:#085041; font-size:0.82rem; margin-bottom:0.5rem;">
        Spending reorientation</div>
      <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.3rem; margin-bottom:1rem;">
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem;">
          <div style="width:25%; background:#0F6E56; height:20px; border-radius:3px; flex-shrink:0;"></div>
          <div style="font-size:0.79rem; color:#085041;">25p+ — industrial capex + ecosystems</div>
        </div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem;">
          <div style="width:15%; background:#1D9E75; height:20px; border-radius:3px; flex-shrink:0;"></div>
          <div style="font-size:0.79rem; color:#085041;">15p — R&amp;D · skilling · standards</div>
        </div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem;">
          <div style="width:12%; background:#5DCAA5; height:20px; border-radius:3px; flex-shrink:0;"></div>
          <div style="font-size:0.79rem; color:#085041;">12p — infrastructure tied to clusters</div>
        </div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem;">
          <div style="width:10%; background:#639922; height:20px; border-radius:3px; flex-shrink:0;"></div>
          <div style="font-size:0.79rem; color:#085041;">10p — education + science (targeted)</div>
        </div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem;">
          <div style="width:8%; background:#378ADD; height:20px; border-radius:3px; flex-shrink:0;"></div>
          <div style="font-size:0.79rem; color:#085041;">8p — welfare (targeted, not universal)</div>
        </div>
      </div>

      <div style="font-weight:600; color:#085041; font-size:0.82rem; margin-bottom:0.5rem;">
        Key structural shifts</div>
      <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.45rem; margin-bottom:1rem;">
        <div style="background:#9FE1CB; border:1px solid #0F6E56; color:#085041;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.45rem 0.6rem; font-size:0.8rem; line-height:1.4;">
          Capex targeted to industrial ecosystems — not just roads</div>
        <div style="background:#9FE1CB; border:1px solid #0F6E56; color:#085041;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.45rem 0.6rem; font-size:0.8rem; line-height:1.4;">
          Reduce repeated utility bailouts — especially power sector</div>
        <div style="background:#9FE1CB; border:1px solid #0F6E56; color:#085041;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.45rem 0.6rem; font-size:0.8rem; line-height:1.4;">
          Tie spending to cluster outcomes — not just headcount</div>
        <div style="background:#9FE1CB; border:1px solid #0F6E56; color:#085041;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.45rem 0.6rem; font-size:0.8rem; line-height:1.4;">
          Supplier parks · worker housing · testing labs · skilling pipelines</div>
        <div style="background:#9FE1CB; border:1px solid #0F6E56; color:#085041;
                    border-radius:6px; padding:0.45rem 0.6rem; font-size:0.8rem; line-height:1.4;">
          Reduce borrowing dependency — grow own-tax base via industry</div>
      </div>

      <div style="font-weight:600; color:#085041; font-size:0.82rem; margin-bottom:0.5rem;">
        How success is measured</div>
      <div style="display:flex; gap:0.5rem;">
        <div style="flex:1; background:#FAECE7; border:1.5px solid #993C1D; color:#712B13;
                    border-radius:8px; padding:0.5rem; font-size:0.78rem;
                    text-align:center; line-height:1.4;">
          <div style="font-weight:600; margin-bottom:0.2rem;">Today</div>
          "₹X crore investment pledged · Y jobs announced"</div>
        <div style="flex:1; background:#E1F5EE; border:1.5px solid #0F6E56; color:#085041;
                    border-radius:8px; padding:0.5rem; font-size:0.78rem;
                    text-align:center; line-height:1.4;">
          <div style="font-weight:600; margin-bottom:0.2rem;">Developmental</div>
          "Z% domestic value addition · sector globally competitive"</div>
      </div>

      <div style="margin-top:0.9rem; background:#1D9E75; border-radius:8px;
                  padding:0.6rem; font-size:0.8rem; color:#04342C;
                  text-align:center; font-weight:600; line-height:1.4;">
        Move from announce-invite-cut-ribbon → allocate capital · build ecosystems · enforce performance
      </div>
    </div>

  </div>

  <div style="display:flex; justify-content:center;">
    <div style="background:#EEEDFE; border:1.5px solid #534AB7; color:#3C3489;
                border-radius:10px; padding:0.7rem 1.6rem; font-size:0.86rem;
                text-align:center; line-height:1.5; max-width:640px;">
      Tamil Nadu's budget is still much more a salary-pension-interest-welfare state
      than a high-capex developmental state
    </div>
  </div>

</div>
```

So Tamil Nadu's budget [@tnbudgetPRS2025] is to fund itself through its own taxes, but it still relies materially on borrowing; and on the spending side, it are still much more a salary-pension-interest-welfare state than a high-capex developmental state.

**Recall how Japan's Government spent its budget [1970]**

For the year 1970[@oecd_japan_1970], “Each 100 yen comes from” picture

4 yen government bonds about 64 yen direct taxes and social security contributions, about 32 yen indirect taxes and other current revenue

Each 100 yen goes to picture:

22 yen local finance
17 yen public works
14 yen social security
12 yen education and science
7 yen defense
4 yen debt service
25 yen other expenditure

So, Japan's spending looked like[@mof_japan_fy2023_finance], little dependence on borrowing, very low debt service, and large weight on public works, education/science, and local finance[@oecd_japan_1970].

**Recall how Taiwan's Government spent its budget**

Taiwan’s fiscal state[@ceicdata_taiwan_budget] was used in a specific way:

1. tax and agrarian extraction to create investible surplus

2. public enterprises to carry large-scale industrial investment

3. fiscal incentives to change sectoral returns

4. and a public sector large enough to steer overall accumulation

So, comparision with India and Tamil Nadu, Taiwan did this, Taiwan used the fiscal state to create investible surplus, steer it into industry, and back that with public enterprises and targeted incentives. 

Roughly in 1975[@ceicdata], this is how they spent their budget:

40 NT dollars national defense
18 NT dollars economic development
11 NT dollars social security and pensions
7 NT dollars general administration
6 NT dollars education, science, and culture
18 NT dollars other expenditure

Taiwan’s developmental budget looked different from Japan’s because of its geopolitical situation: defense was very large, but even so, a very substantial share was still going directly to economic development, with education and social spending rising over time.

```{=html}
<div style="font-family:sans-serif; padding:1rem 0;">
  <div style="display:flex; justify-content:center; margin-bottom:0.8rem;">
    <div style="background:#2C2C2A; border:2px solid #444441; color:#F1EFE8;
                border-radius:10px; padding:0.6rem 2rem; text-align:center;">
      <div style="font-weight:700; font-size:1rem;">Where does each rupee/yen/dollar go?</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.82rem; color:#D3D1C7;">Budget composition reveals developmental ambition</div>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div style="display:flex; gap:0.8rem; margin-bottom:0.8rem;">
    <div style="flex:1; border:2.5px solid #185FA5; border-radius:14px; background:#E6F1FB; padding:1rem;">
      <div style="font-weight:700; color:#0C447C; font-size:0.9rem; margin-bottom:0.2rem;">India 2026–27</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.75rem; color:#185FA5; margin-bottom:0.7rem; font-style:italic;">Central government budget</div>
      <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.35rem;">
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.4rem;"><div style="width:22%; background:#E24B4A; height:18px; border-radius:3px;"></div><div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#0C447C;">22p — states' share of taxes</div></div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.4rem;"><div style="width:20%; background:#D85A30; height:18px; border-radius:3px;"></div><div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#0C447C;">20p — interest payments</div></div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.4rem;"><div style="width:17%; background:#BA7517; height:18px; border-radius:3px;"></div><div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#0C447C;">17p — central sector schemes</div></div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.4rem;"><div style="width:11%; background:#378ADD; height:18px; border-radius:3px;"></div><div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#0C447C;">11p — defence</div></div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.4rem;"><div style="width:8%; background:#639922; height:18px; border-radius:3px;"></div><div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#0C447C;">8p — centrally sponsored schemes</div></div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.4rem;"><div style="width:6%; background:#1D9E75; height:18px; border-radius:3px;"></div><div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#0C447C;">6p — major subsidies</div></div>
      </div>
      <div style="margin-top:0.7rem; background:#FAECE7; border:1px solid #993C1D; border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.78rem; color:#712B13; text-align:center;">
        Dominated by transfers, debt service, committed expenditure</div>
    </div>
    <div style="flex:1; border:2.5px solid #0F6E56; border-radius:14px; background:#F0FAF5; padding:1rem;">
      <div style="font-weight:700; color:#085041; font-size:0.9rem; margin-bottom:0.2rem;">Japan 1970</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.75rem; color:#0F6E56; margin-bottom:0.7rem; font-style:italic;">Peak developmental state era</div>
      <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.35rem;">
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.4rem;"><div style="width:22%; background:#1D9E75; height:18px; border-radius:3px;"></div><div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#085041;">22y — local finance</div></div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.4rem;"><div style="width:17%; background:#0F6E56; height:18px; border-radius:3px;"></div><div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#085041;">17y — public works</div></div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.4rem;"><div style="width:14%; background:#639922; height:18px; border-radius:3px;"></div><div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#085041;">14y — social security</div></div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.4rem;"><div style="width:12%; background:#3B6D11; height:18px; border-radius:3px;"></div><div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#085041;">12y — education and science</div></div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.4rem;"><div style="width:4%; background:#888780; height:18px; border-radius:3px;"></div><div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#085041;">4y — debt service only</div></div>
      </div>
      <div style="margin-top:0.7rem; background:#E1F5EE; border:1px solid #0F6E56; border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.78rem; color:#085041; text-align:center;">
        Low debt service · large public works + education weight</div>
    </div>
    <div style="flex:1; border:2.5px solid #854F0B; border-radius:14px; background:#FAEEDA; padding:1rem;">
      <div style="font-weight:700; color:#633806; font-size:0.9rem; margin-bottom:0.2rem;">Taiwan 1975</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.75rem; color:#854F0B; margin-bottom:0.7rem; font-style:italic;">High-growth developmental era</div>
      <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.35rem;">
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.4rem;"><div style="width:40%; background:#888780; height:18px; border-radius:3px;"></div><div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#633806;">40NT — national defence</div></div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.4rem;"><div style="width:18%; background:#854F0B; height:18px; border-radius:3px;"></div><div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#633806;">18NT — economic development</div></div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.4rem;"><div style="width:11%; background:#EF9F27; height:18px; border-radius:3px;"></div><div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#633806;">11NT — social security + pensions</div></div>
        <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.4rem;"><div style="width:6%; background:#639922; height:18px; border-radius:3px;"></div><div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#633806;">6NT — education, science, culture</div></div>
      </div>
      <div style="margin-top:0.7rem; background:#FAEEDA; border:1px solid #854F0B; border-radius:6px; padding:0.4rem; font-size:0.78rem; color:#633806; text-align:center;">
        Substantial economic development share despite large defence</div>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div style="display:flex; justify-content:center;">
    <div style="background:#EEEDFE; border:1.5px solid #534AB7; color:#3C3489;
                border-radius:10px; padding:0.6rem 1.4rem; font-size:0.84rem; text-align:center;">
      India's fiscal state is still dominated by transfers and debt service — not concentrated industrial strategy
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Building India's Political class

None of this fiscal reorientation is possible without a political class capable of sustaining long-horizon decisions.
So, India and states of India like Tamil Nadu needs to build a Political Class India Deserves. 

The country asks for competence from its engineers, doctors, civil servants, judges, and scientists. 
It does not ask the same of those who direct them. So, We as Indians need to demand that democracy works better.
This is adding accountability installed in political office and power genuinely distributed to citizens to evaluate and remove their representatives on the basis of performance.

Indian Political entry depends too much on family power, caste arithmetic, money, factional loyalty, celebrity, and raw winnability.
However, in a democratic society, any Indian is qualified to enter the public office, serve people of India. 
Currently, Parties often function like private property rather than public institutions.
In reality, Political party's are public institutions and belong to the people. 

```{=html}
<div style="font-family:sans-serif; padding:1rem 0;">

  <div style="display:flex; justify-content:center; margin-bottom:0.8rem;">
    <div style="background:#2C2C2A; border:2px solid #444441; color:#F1EFE8;
                border-radius:12px; padding:0.9rem 2.4rem; text-align:center; max-width:600px;">
      <div style="font-weight:700; font-size:1.05rem; letter-spacing:0.01em; margin-bottom:0.3rem;">
        Building the political class India deserves</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.85rem; color:#D3D1C7;">
        Five reforms · each with a measurable outcome</div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.6rem;">

    <div style="display:flex; gap:0.8rem; align-items:stretch;">
      <div style="width:40px; display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center;
                  background:#534AB7; border-radius:8px; color:#EEEDFE;
                  font-weight:700; font-size:1rem; flex-shrink:0;">
        1</div>
      <div style="flex:1; border:1.5px solid #534AB7; border-radius:10px;
                  background:#EEEDFE; padding:0.8rem 1rem; display:flex; gap:1rem; align-items:center;">
        <div style="flex:1.4;">
          <div style="font-weight:700; color:#3C3489; font-size:0.88rem;
                      margin-bottom:0.3rem; line-height:1.4;">
            Qualifying exams + training</div>
          <div style="font-size:0.8rem; color:#534AB7; line-height:1.5;">
            MLA · MP · mayor candidates pass a public leadership exam covering
            the Constitution, budgets, legal responsibility, and ethics</div>
        </div>
        <div style="flex:1; background:#CECBF6; border-radius:8px; padding:0.6rem 0.8rem;
                    font-size:0.8rem; color:#3C3489; line-height:1.5; text-align:center;">
          Politics becomes a profession with minimum standards — candidates understand
          how the state works before they enter it</div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div style="display:flex; gap:0.8rem; align-items:stretch;">
      <div style="width:40px; display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center;
                  background:#0F6E56; border-radius:8px; color:#E1F5EE;
                  font-weight:700; font-size:1rem; flex-shrink:0;">
        2</div>
      <div style="flex:1; border:1.5px solid #0F6E56; border-radius:10px;
                  background:#F0FAF5; padding:0.8rem 1rem; display:flex; gap:1rem; align-items:center;">
        <div style="flex:1.4;">
          <div style="font-weight:700; color:#085041; font-size:0.88rem;
                      margin-bottom:0.3rem; line-height:1.4;">
            Political Job Performance</div>
          <div style="font-size:0.8rem; color:#0F6E56; line-height:1.5;">
            Attendance · funds spent · promises kept · criminal cases · asset growth ·
            grievances resolved — with a legal mechanism to initiate review or removal</div>
        </div>
        <div style="flex:1; background:#9FE1CB; border-radius:8px; padding:0.6rem 0.8rem;
                    font-size:0.8rem; color:#085041; line-height:1.5; text-align:center;">
          Politics shifts from speeches and symbolism to measurable delivery —
          politicians know they can be judged and removed for incompetence</div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div style="display:flex; gap:0.8rem; align-items:stretch;">
      <div style="width:40px; display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center;
                  background:#185FA5; border-radius:8px; color:#E6F1FB;
                  font-weight:700; font-size:1rem; flex-shrink:0;">
        3</div>
      <div style="flex:1; border:1.5px solid #185FA5; border-radius:10px;
                  background:#E6F1FB; padding:0.8rem 1rem; display:flex; gap:1rem; align-items:center;">
        <div style="flex:1.4;">
          <div style="font-weight:700; color:#0C447C; font-size:0.88rem;
                      margin-bottom:0.3rem; line-height:1.4;">
            Internal party democracy</div>
          <div style="font-size:0.8rem; color:#185FA5; line-height:1.5;">
            Mandatory internal elections · disclosed finances · transparent candidate
            selection criteria · limits on family domination · open career paths</div>
        </div>
        <div style="flex:1; background:#B5D4F4; border-radius:8px; padding:0.6rem 0.8rem;
                    font-size:0.8rem; color:#0C447C; line-height:1.5; text-align:center;">
          More talented people enter politics without needing family background —
          parties become institutions, not private empires</div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div style="display:flex; gap:0.8rem; align-items:stretch;">
      <div style="width:40px; display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center;
                  background:#854F0B; border-radius:8px; color:#FAEEDA;
                  font-weight:700; font-size:1rem; flex-shrink:0;">
        4</div>
      <div style="flex:1; border:1.5px solid #854F0B; border-radius:10px;
                  background:#FAEEDA; padding:0.8rem 1rem; display:flex; gap:1rem; align-items:center;">
        <div style="flex:1.4;">
          <div style="font-weight:700; color:#633806; font-size:0.88rem;
                      margin-bottom:0.3rem; line-height:1.4;">
            Depoliticize major public appointments</div>
          <div style="font-size:0.8rem; color:#854F0B; line-height:1.5;">
            Vice-chancellors · regulators · utility heads · hospital boards · public
            corporation leaders selected through transparent merit based processes</div>
        </div>
        <div style="flex:1; background:#FAC775; border-radius:8px; padding:0.6rem 0.8rem;
                    font-size:0.8rem; color:#633806; line-height:1.5; text-align:center;">
          Universities, boards, and public institutions perform better —
          institutional decay reduces as leadership quality improves</div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div style="display:flex; gap:0.8rem; align-items:stretch;">
      <div style="width:40px; display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center;
                  background:#993C1D; border-radius:8px; color:#FAECE7;
                  font-weight:700; font-size:1rem; flex-shrink:0;">
        5</div>
      <div style="flex:1; border:1.5px solid #993C1D; border-radius:10px;
                  background:#FAECE7; padding:0.8rem 1rem; display:flex; gap:1rem; align-items:center;">
        <div style="flex:1.4;">
          <div style="font-weight:700; color:#712B13; font-size:0.88rem;
                      margin-bottom:0.3rem; line-height:1.4;">
            New entry routes into public life</div>
          <div style="font-size:0.8rem; color:#993C1D; line-height:1.5;">
            Political fellowships · municipal academies · legislative apprenticeships ·
            district policy programs · open to diaspora and professionals nationwide</div>
        </div>
        <div style="flex:1; background:#F5C4B3; border-radius:8px; padding:0.6rem 0.8rem;
                    font-size:0.8rem; color:#712B13; line-height:1.5; text-align:center;">
          Politics becomes accessible to serious people from ordinary backgrounds —
          not just dynasties, caste networks, or patronage systems</div>
      </div>
    </div>

  </div>

  <div style="display:flex; justify-content:center; margin-top:0.8rem;">
    <div style="background:#EAF3DE; border:1.5px solid #3B6D11; color:#27500A;
                border-radius:10px; padding:0.7rem 1.6rem; font-size:0.86rem;
                text-align:center; line-height:1.5; max-width:600px;">
      Governance shifts from patronage to performance — slowly but irreversibly
    </div>
  </div>

</div>
```

### 1. Introduce qualifying exams and training for politicians

Anyone seeking to contest for MLA, MP, mayor, or major local offices should pass a basic public leadership exam. 
The exam should test practical knowledge, the reason is because, to ensure that anyone seeking serious public office understands the Constitution, budgets, and legal responsibility. 

MLAs, MPs, and other members of India’s political class exercise some of the highest authority in public life. 
They make laws, allocate resources, oversee institutions, and shape the lives of millions. 
Because they carry such enormous responsibility, they should be among the most competent people in the country. A system that demands standards from engineers, doctors, judges, and civil servants should not exempt those who govern them.

Constitution and rights
Public finance and budgets
Local government powers
Education, health, and infrastructure basics
Ethics and corruption law
Administrative process and public problem-solving

After passing, candidates should undergo formal training before entering office.

**Outcome: Politics becomes a profession with minimum standards.**
Candidates will at least understand how the state works, how money is spent, and what their legal responsibilities are

### 2. Give citizens the power to monitor and punish poor performance

Every elected politician should have a public performance dashboard:

attendance,
funds received and spent,
promises made,
works completed,
criminal cases,
asset growth,
constituency grievances resolved.

Voters should also have a legal mechanism to initiate review, and in some levels of government, even remove persistently non-performing representatives.

**Outcome: Politics shifts from speeches and symbolism to measurable delivery.**
Politicians will know they can be judged and punished for incompetence

### 3. Force internal democracy inside political parties

Parties should be required to, hold internal elections, disclose finances, publish candidate selection criteria, limit family domination,
create transparent career paths for workers and professionals.

**Outcome: More talented people can enter politics without needing family background or loyalty to a local boss.**
Parties become institutions, not private empires

### 4. Depoliticize appointments to major public institutions

Vice-chancellors, regulators, utility heads, hospital boards, and public corporation leaders should be selected through transparent and merit-based processes, not party loyalty.

**Outcome: Universities, boards, and public institutions will perform better because leadership quality improves.**
Institutional decay will reduce

### 5. Build new entry routes into public life

India and Tamil Nadu should create, political fellowships, municipal leadership academies, legislative apprenticeships, district public policy programs.

A capable graduate from Madurai, Salem, Coimbatore, or Tirunelveli or even Indians who are abroad,  should be able to enter politics through service, training, and open competition. They bring skillsets that might be highly valuable. 

**Outcome: Politics becomes accessible to serious people from ordinary backgrounds** 
This will remove current access to politics from just dynasties or patronage networks

### What would happen if this is implemented?

If these reforms are implemented seriously, the outcomes would be clear:

The average quality of politicians would rise, parties would have to recruit more capable people, politics would begin to attract professionals, reformers, and problem-solvers, governance would slowly shift from patronage to performance and people-centric.
